title: Generation and time evolution of anomalous Floquet Majorana flat edge modes in two-dimensional noncolinear magnet-superconductor heterostructures
authors: Kamalesh Bera, Priyanka Mohan, Arijit Saha
abstract: We theoretically investigate the realization of gapless Floquet topological superconducting phases in a two-dimensional magnet-superconductor heterostructure (2D Shiba lattice) in the presence of a harmonic drive implemented in the chemical potential. Employing a real-space tight-binding model, we obtain both the regular $0$- and anomalous $ŽĆ$-Floquet Majorana flat edge modes (FMFEMs) in the quasi-energy spectrum. We also study the real-time evolution of the FMFEMs and analyze their local density of states in the presence of such a periodic drive. The topological characterization is performed using the winding number, exploiting the chiral symmetry of the equivalent bulk effective momentum-space Hamiltonian. This is also supported by the corresponding edge state spectra. Furthermore, we employ the Brillouin-Wigner (BW) and Floquet perturbation theory (FPT) to gain analytical insight into the problem. We compare our exact (numerical), BW, and FPT results in terms of the quasi-energy spectra obtained across different frequency regimes. We find good agreement between the exact numerical, BW, and FPT results in the higher-frequency and high-amplitude domain, particularly close to the $0$-quasi-energy modes.
summary: This theoretical work explores how periodically driving a magnet-superconductor junction can generate exotic topological superconducting phases characterized by anomalous Majorana flat edge modes. By analyzing the quasi-energy spectrum and winding number across varying driving frequencies, the authors map out a topological phase diagram. The findings demonstrate the tunability of these topological states using time-periodic driving, which has implications for superconducting quantum devices.
main_problem: The study investigates the generation and time evolution of anomalous Floquet Majorana flat edge modes (FMFEMs) in a 2D noncollinear magnet-superconductor heterostructure subjected to a harmonic drive.
main_result: The system exhibits distinct topological phases dependent on the driving frequency, characterized by the emergence of $\pi$-FMFEMs at low frequencies and $0$-FMFEMs at high frequencies, confirmed by the winding number.
method: The research employs a real-space tight-binding model analyzed using Floquet theory, supplemented by Brillouin-Wigner and Floquet perturbation theory for analytical insight.
model_or_system: The physical system is a 2D Shiba lattice (magnet-superconductor heterostructure) described by a Bogoliubov-de-Gennes Hamiltonian, which is periodically driven by a time-dependent chemical potential.
key_observables: Quasi-energy spectrum, Local Density of States (LDOS), and the topological winding number.
important_parameters: Driving frequency ($\Omega$), driving amplitude ($V$), and the superconducting gap ($\Delta_0$). Key regimes include low, intermediate, and high frequencies.
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on the validity of the Floquet formalism, noting that perturbative methods (BW/FPT) fail to capture $\pi$-FMFEMs in the low-frequency regime.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the edge spectra (quasi-energy vs. momentum) for different driving frequencies, and show the winding number as a function of momentum to confirm topological invariants.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from defining the physical model and Hamiltonian to calculating the quasi-energy spectrum across different driving regimes, followed by topological characterization using the winding number and comparison with perturbative expansions.
why_interesting: The use of Floquet engineering to induce and control topological superconductivity, particularly the emergence of anomalous $\pi$-modes, is highly relevant to open quantum systems and superconducting quantum circuits.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic representation of a 2D non-collinear spin texture decorated on a square lattice and deposited on the surface of a common s-wave SC. A time-periodic harmonic drive is applied to the onsite chemical potential to realize 0- and anomalous ŽĆ-FMFEMs.

FIG. 2. In panels (a) and (c), we depict the quasi-energy spectra as a function of the state index m for the driven system, choosing the driving frequency Ōä”= 5Ōłå0 and Ōä”= 10Ōłå0 respectively. The red color dots indicate 0- and ŽĆ-FMFEMs. In panel (b), we illustrate the LDOS distribution of ŽĆ-FMFEMs (corresponding to the panel (a)) localized at the edges of the 2D domain. Similarly, in panel (d) we illustrate the LDOS spectra corresponding to the 0-FMFEMs in panel (c). The other model parameters are chosen as Ōłå0 = t = 1, J = 2Ōłå0, V = 5Ōłå0, gx = gy = ŽĆ/2.

FIG. 3. In panels (a), (b), and (c), we demonstrate the time-dependent eigenvalue spectra E(t) of the time-evolution operator U(t, 0) as a function of time t over a full driving period choosing Ōä”= 5Ōłå0, 10Ōłå0, and 15Ōłå0, respectively. Panels (d)ŌĆō(f) show the evolution of the edge states corresponding to the 0-FMFEMs at intermediate frequency (Ōä”= 10Ōłå0), considering time scales t = 0.5T, 0.75T, and 0.98T, respectively. The same is depicted for the ŽĆ-FMFEMs in panels (g)ŌĆō(i) considering the same time instants mentioned above. The other model parameters are chosen as Ōłå0 = t = 1, J = 2Ōłå0, V = 5Ōłå0, and gx = gy = ŽĆ/2.

FIG. 4. Panels (a)-(c) correspond to the quasi-energy edge spectra shown as a function of momentum kx for three different values of the driving frequency, Ōä”= 5Ōłå0, 10Ōłå0, and 15Ōłå0, respectively. On the other hand, panels (d)-(f) exhibit the corresponding winding number as a function of momentum kx for the same choice of respective driving frequencies. The other model parameters are chosen as follows: V = 5Ōłå0, J = 2Ōłå0, Ōłå0 = t = 1, and gx = gy = ŽĆ/2.

FIG. 5. Density plots of the winding number (evaluated at kx = 0) is displayed in the plane of driving amplitude (V ) and frequency (Ōä”). We present the phase diagram for two different values of the exchange coupling: J = 1.6Ōłå0 (panel (a)) and J = 2Ōłå0 (panel (b)), corresponding to the trivial and topological phases of the static system, respectively. The other model parameters are chosen as Ōłå0 = t = 1, V = 5Ōłå0, and gx = gy = ŽĆ/2.
